author: Nick
description: Drift-proof, closed-corpus citations via block refs. Cite only from your clipped sources — fabrication impossible by construction. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Nick
manifest.description: Catch quotes that silently went stale. When you edit a source note, Cite Engine flags any quote you pulled from it that no longer matches, so your literature notes and citations stay accurate.
manifest.name: Cite Engine

name: Cite Engine

releases.author: Flyingmiata-droid
releases.description: Plain language pass, no code changes from 0.0.4. Rewrote the description and readme so the plugin is findable by the words people actually use (quotes going stale, literature notes, source notes, zotero). Same three commands, same behavior.

releases.author: Flyingmiata-droid
releases.description: Cite Engine 0.0.4. Fixes an irregular-whitespace lint flag (a BOM literal in a regex, now an escape) caught in the community review. No behavior change. Install via Community Plugins, BRAT (Flyingmiata-droid/cite-engine), or manually with main.js and manifest.json.

releases.author: Flyingmiata-droid
releases.description:
Cite Engine 0.0.3. Drift-proof, closed-corpus citations for Obsidian. Cite only from your own clipped sources; fabrication is impossible by construction, and the integrity check flags any quote that has silently gone stale against its source.

Fix: Integrity check no longer errors when re-run (report is now create-or-modify). README polished for end users.

Install via Community Plugins, BRAT (Flyingmiata-droid/cite-engine), or manually with main.js and manifest.json.

releases.author: Flyingmiata-droid
releases.description: Onboarding fix: the three commands are now always visible in the command palette (previously hidden unless a note was focused), plus a ribbon icon for the Cite action. No change to the citation/integrity logic. Install via BRAT: Flyingmiata-droid/cite-engine.
